---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "log1p"
namespace: "Transform"
description: "Logarithmize the data matrix."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `log1p`  
Namespace: `transform`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/transform/log1p)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/transform/log1p)

:::

Computes X = log(X + 1), where log denotes the natural logarithm unless a different base is given

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/transform/log1p/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/transform/log1p).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/transform/log1p/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`              |Input h5mu file                                                                                                |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"`  |
|`--modality`           |Which modality from the input MuData file to process.                                                          |`string`, default: `"rna"`                 |
|`--input_layer`        |Input layer to use. If None, X is normalized                                                                   |`string`                                   |
|`--output_layer`       |Output layer to use. By default, use X.                                                                        |`string`                                   |
|`--output`             |Output h5mu file.                                                                                              |`file`, required, default: `"output.h5mu"` |
|`--base`               |Base of the logarithm. Natural logarithm is used by default.                                                   |`double`, example: `2`                     |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                |

## Authors


  * Dries De Maeyer [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/ddemaeyer) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-de-maeyer-b46a814) (maintainer)

  * Robrecht Cannoodt [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/rcannood) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3641-729X) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/robrechtcannoodt) (contributor)
